note: Advisory sandbox bridge. Injects CHESS forecast findings as extra REPAIR_CANDIDATE-style packet-lint reasons. Emits CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED, never a bare governance PASS. Read-only over the packet; standalone — nothing downstream is BLOCKED by it.
title: CHESS -> packet-lint bridge (inject CHESS forecast findings as extra packet-lint reasons)

subject.note: linter imported and lint_packet() called; CHESS run as its real CLI over the packet's task+context_summary as raw_move; both ground tools used as-is (no reimplementation). Packet files read-only, sha unchanged.

finding.detail: Real on-disk packet P0PGR-20260708-010 (task text contains 'clean') -> CHESS returns likely_misread + second_move_risk -> bridge injects 2 extra REPAIR_CANDIDATE reasons and flags CHECK_REJECTED. Real packet P0PGR-20260708-007 ('spend') -> CHESS action=ASK_IF_IRREVERSIBLE -> injected via the action branch. Real packet P0PGR-20260708-002 (no risk words) -> CHESS-clean, zero injected reasons, CHECK_OK.
finding.interpretation: CHESS forecast risk latent in real dispatch packets is surfaced onto the packet-lint result as extra repair candidates. Reported as a finding, not relaxed away; the bridge blocks nothing (advisory, standalone).

what_it_proves: REAL plain packet P0PGR-20260708-002 (no CHESS risk words) -> CHECK_OK with ZERO CHESS reasons injected (positive: accepted)

a MINIMAL MUTATION of that same real packet (append 'clean / simplify / delete' to its task) -> CHECK_REJECTED with CHESS likely_misread reasons injected (RED-before-GREEN: green packet made red by the injected wording)
REAL packet P0PGR-20260708-010 (naturally says 'clean') is flagged as-is -> CHECK_REJECTED via likely_misread (real artifact, no strawman)
REAL packet P0PGR-20260708-007 (says 'spend') is flagged via the action=ASK_IF_IRREVERSIBLE branch even with empty likely_misread
POSITIVE CONTROL: a 'delete/simplify' string MUST make CHESS emit reasons, so a zero-hit on the plain packet cannot be a broken/always-clean detector
bridge ADDS CHESS reasons on top of the lint result (never drops a base lint reason); reasons == lint_reasons + injected_chess_reasons
running the bridge leaves the packet file byte-identical (sha unchanged) and never mutates the in-memory packet dict (read-only)
verdict vocab CHECK_OK/CHECK_REJECTED, never a bare governance PASS (the linter's own PASS/REPAIR_CANDIDATE is preserved verbatim as lint_verdict); detector exits NONZERO on a CHESS hit, 0 when clean
chess_patches_applied: runs the REAL chess_pass_cli and REAL lint_packet over REAL packets (no reimplementation, no strawman)
negative fixture is a minimal mutation of a real packet's task text; positive is the real packet as-is — no invariant relaxation
reads the packet, never writes to it (Lock 5 / read-only); advisory only, downstream_blocked=false
origin=sandbox-advisory / authority=none; injects CHESS reasons as a finding, never its own governance PASS
locks_respected: Lock 5 append-only (packet fixtures unmodified, sha unchanged)
no live call (chess/lint run locally over temp input; no network, no Supabase, no send/deploy)
DRAFT-only posture: bridge is standalone advisory, blocks nothing, sets no dispatch flags
worktree not pushed; wrote only inside catalog/builds/BR-3/
exit_gate_B3: BR-3 done: CHESS->packet-lint bridge injects real CHESS forecast findings as extra REPAIR_CANDIDATE reasons; RED-before-GREEN via minimal-mutation of a real plain packet + real 'clean'/'spend' packets flagged as-is; positive-control guards against a broken detector.
next_action: None required — advisory bridge, standalone. Optionally wire as a non-blocking advisory step alongside the packet linter.
